pageDescription: Calculate your Basal Metabolic Rate (BMR) to determine the number of calories your body needs at rest for basic physiological functions

definition.title: Basal Metabolic Rate Definition
definition.description: BMR is the number of calories your body needs to maintain basic physiological functions at rest

components.title: BMR Components
components.description: BMR includes breathing, circulation, cell production, and basic organ function

factors.title: Factors Affecting BMR
factors.description: Age, gender, weight, height, muscle mass, and genetics all influence BMR

mifflin.title: Mifflin-St Jeor Equation
mifflin.description: Considered the most accurate for healthy individuals

harris.title: Harris-Benedict Equation
harris.description: One of the oldest and still widely used BMR equations

criticalWarningsList.notDiagnostic: This calculator provides BMR estimates only
criticalWarningsList.individual: BMR varies based on individual factors and health conditions

importantNotesList: BMR represents calories needed at complete rest only
Actual daily needs are higher when including activity and exercise
Muscle mass increases BMR as muscle tissue burns more calories than fat
BMR decreases with age due to loss of muscle mass and metabolic changes

messages.enterInformation: Enter your information to calculate Basal Metabolic Rate

disclaimer.title: Important Disclaimer
disclaimer.content: This calculator provides BMR estimates based on mathematical formulas. Individual BMR may vary based on factors not accounted for in these calculations, including muscle mass, health conditions, and genetics.

seekHelp.title: Nutritional Resources
seekHelp.content: For personalized metabolic assessments and nutrition advice, consult healthcare professionals or registered dietitians with expertise in metabolic testing and individual health needs.

errors.heightCm: Please enter valid height in cm

tooltips.calculationMethod: Different equations may produce slightly different results
tooltips.gender: Gender affects BMR due to differences in body composition

enter_your_details_to_calculate_your_bas: Enter your details to calculate your Basal Metabolic Rate
required_for_katchmcardle_formula_use_de: Required for Katch-McArdle formula. Use DEXA scan or body fat calipers for accuracy.
enter_your_details_and_click_calculate_s: Enter your details and click Calculate see your BMR and daily calorie needs.
this_is_the_number_of_calories_your_body:
This is the number of calories your body burns at rest to maintain basic physiological
                          functions.
bmr_370_216_lbm_where_lbm_1_body_fat_100: BMR = 370 + (21.6 × LBM), where LBM = (1 - Body Fat % / 100) × Weight
best_for_lean_individuals_with_known_bod: Best for lean individuals with known body fat %
bmr_decreases_with_age_and_increases_wit: BMR decreases with age and increases with muscle mass
these_are_estimates_individual_metabolis: These are estimates - individual metabolism varies
for_weight_loss_create_a_moderate_calori: For weight loss, create a moderate calorie deficit
consult_healthcare_providers_for_persona: Consult healthcare providers for personalized advice
